articles.title: Boston Marathon runner honors sister’s recovery from severe crash
articles.description:
After a severe car accident, Michael Chiang's sister relearned to walk — and ran a 5K. Now he's running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation.

The post Boston Marathon runner honors sister’s recovery from severe crash appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Michael Chiang will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his sister's recovery.
His sister relearned to walk after a severe car accident and completed a 5K.
Funds raised will support Spaulding Rehabilitation, benefiting local Boston residents.

articles.title: Republicans are launching a voting bill debate that could last days or even weeks
articles.description:
The talkathon could last a week or longer, potentially through the weekend, as Senate Majority Leader John Thune tries to navigate Trump’s insistence on the issue and Democrats’ united opposition.

The post Republicans are launching a voting bill debate that could last days or even weeks appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Senate debate on voting bill may extend through the weekend, affecting legislative schedule.
Senate Majority Leader John Thune leads discussions amid Trump's insistence on voting reforms.
Boston residents could face delays in local governance due to prolonged Senate discussions.

articles.title: Body pulled from Wakefield lake believed to be that of missing man
articles.description:
Jason Thomas, 45, was reported missing on Dec. 13, hours after he left his home in Wakefield and failed to return.

The post Body pulled from Wakefield lake believed to be that of missing man appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Body recovered from Wakefield lake identified as Jason Thomas, 45, missing since December 13.
Thomas left his home in Wakefield before disappearing, raising community concerns.
Local authorities continue to investigate circumstances surrounding his disappearance.

articles.title: Massachusetts online casino bill sent to study, effectively shelved for now
articles.description:
Proposal to legalize digital slot machines and blackjack is unlikely to advance this year.

The post Massachusetts online casino bill sent to study, effectively shelved for now appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Massachusetts online casino bill has been sent for further study, delaying legalization efforts.
Proposal aimed to legalize digital slot machines and blackjack, but unlikely to advance in 2023.
Boston residents will not see new online gambling options this year, impacting local revenue.

articles.title: Police: Bus driver in Medford crash may have been asleep at the wheel
articles.description:
Investigators “do not believe alcohol and or other intoxicating substances is a factor” in the March 8 crash, MBTA Transit Police said.

The post Police: Bus driver in Medford crash may have been asleep at the wheel appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Investigators reported the MBTA bus driver may have been asleep during the March 8 crash.
No evidence of alcohol or drugs was found in the investigation by MBTA Transit Police.
The incident raises concerns about driver fatigue and safety for Boston commuters.

articles.title: Black Mountain in N.H. will not become a community co-op as planned
articles.description:
It will become a laboratory for solving the challenges facing independent ski areas.

The post Black Mountain in N.H. will not become a community co-op as planned appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Black Mountain in New Hampshire will not become a community co-op as planned.
The area will instead serve as a laboratory for independent ski area challenges.
Boston residents may see changes in ski options and community engagement in the region.

articles.title: Why a 35-year-old breast cancer survivor is running the Boston Marathon
articles.description:
The 2026 Boston Marathon will be Julia Aldrich's fourth marathon, but first for charity. Why she's running for the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.

The post Why a 35-year-old breast cancer survivor is running the Boston Marathon appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Julia Aldrich, a 35-year-old breast cancer survivor, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon.
This will be her fourth marathon and first to raise funds for charity.
Proceeds will support the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, benefiting local cancer research.
